sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to formula foods for use in very low energy diets for weight reduction as defined in Section 2. These foods are defined as foods for special medical purposes and must be used under medical supervision by individuals with moderate or severe obesity. The matter of sale on prescription should be a decision made at national level. It does not apply to pre-packaged meals presented in the form of conventional foods.

sections.title: DEFINITION
sections.text: A formula food for use in very low energy diets is a food specially prepared to supply a minimum amount of carbohydrates and the daily requirements of the essential nutrients in 450–800 kcal, which represents the sole source of energy intake.

sections.title: Protein
sections.text: Not less than 50 g protein with a nutritional qualityi equivalent to a protein-digestibility-corrected amino acid score of 1 shall be present in the recommended daily intake of energy. Essential amino acids may be added to improve protein quality only in amounts necessary for this purpose. Only L-forms of amino acids shall be used, except that DL-methionine may be used.

sections.title: Vitamins and minerals
sections.text: Very low energy diets shall provide 100 percent of the recommended daily intakes for vitamins and minerals. Other essential nutrients not specified below may also be included. Vitaminsii Vitamin A 600 g Vitamin D 2.5 g Vitamin E 10 mg Vitamin C 30 mg Thiamine 0.8 mg Riboflavin 1.2 mg Niacin 11 mg Vitamin B6 2 mg Vitamin B12 1 g Folic Acid (as monoglutamate) 200 g Minerals Calcium 500 mg i Report of the Joint FAO/WHO Expert Consultation on Protein Quality Evaluation, Bethesda, MD USA, 4 – 8 December 1989, FAO Food and Nutrition Paper No. 51, 1991, Rome, p. 23. ii These lists should be reviewed when new FAO/WHO recommendations become available.

sections.title: Ingredients
sections.text: Very low energy diets shall be prepared from protein constituents of animal and/or plant which have been proved suitable for human consumption and from other suitable ingredients necessary to achieve the essential composition of the product as set out in Sections 3.1 and 3.2 above.

sections.title: Pesticide residues
sections.text: The product shall be prepared with special care under good manufacturing practices, so that residues of pesticides, which may be required in the production, storage or processing of the raw materials or the finished food ingredient, do not remain in the product, or, if technically unavoidable, are reduced to the maximum extent possible, and shall comply with those maximum residue limits established by the Codex Committee on Pesticide Residues for this commodity.

sections.title: FILL OF CONTAINER
sections.text:
In the case of products in ready-to-eat form, the fill of the container shall be:
(a) not less than 80 percent v/v for products weighing less than 150 g (5 oz);
(b) not less than 85 percent v/v for products in the weight range of 150–250 g (5–8 oz); and
(c) not less than 90 percent v/v for products weighing more than 250 g (8 oz) of the water capacity of the container. The water capacity of the container is the volume of distilled water at 20 °C which the sealed container will hold when completely filled.

sections.title: Information for utilization
sections.retrieval_text:
LABELLING > Information for utilization
number=9.6
level=2
In addition to the appropriate sections of the Standard on the Labelling of and Claims for Foods for Special Medical Purposes (CXS 180-1991), the following directions should be provided:
- the statement "for the dietary management of obesity" shall be declared on the label, in close proximity to the name of the food;
- reference to the importance of maintaining adequate daily fluid intake;
- a statement that the product should not be used by pregnant, nursing and lactating women or by infants, children, adolescents and elderly, except when medically indicated.

sections.title: Unopened food
sections.text: Any special conditions for the storage of the food shall be declared on the label if the validity of the date depends thereon. Storage instructions of opened packages of the food shall be included on the label to ensure that the opened food maintains its wholesomeness and nutritive value. A warning should be included on the label if the food is not capable of being stored after opening or is not capable of being stored in the container after opening.

sections.title: Additional provisions
sections.text: A statement that the product may not be recommended for use for purposes other than the dietary management of obesity. The statements with respect to the name of the food and the indications for use as given in Sections 9.1 and 9.6 shall appear on the label of the package and/or sachet for use by the consumer. Other statements, as required under Section 9.6 above and Section 4.5 of the Standard for the Labelling of and Claims for Foods for Special Medical Purposes (CXS 180-1991), may appear on an accompanying leaflet in which case reference shall be made to this fact on the label of the package and/or sachet.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR FORMULA FOODS FOR USE IN VERY LOW ENERGY DIETS FOR WEIGHT REDUCTION
pages.summary: Página contém apenas o título principal do documento e cabeçalho de padrão, sem seções hierárquicas ou texto interno para mapear.
Página apresenta cabeçalho padrão e texto explicativo contínuo, sem títulos estruturais ou seções numeradas. Nada mapeado para hierarquia.

Página apresenta 3 seções principais numeradas (1, 2, 3) com textos explicativos associados. A seção 3 possui subtítulos numerados até nível 3, detalhando composição e qualidade dos alimentos para dietas muito baixas em energia. Nenhuma seção continua de página anterior ou para a próxima página.

Página apresenta 5 seções principais numeradas (3.3, 4, 5, 6, 7) com subtítulos e subsubtítulos numerados até nível 3, detalhando ingredientes, aditivos alimentares, contaminantes, higiene e embalagem. Textos associados estão corretamente alocados, com estrutura clara e sem continuidade entre páginas.

Página contém as seções principais 8 e 9 representando 'FILL OF CONTAINER' e 'LABELLING' respectivamente. A seção 9 apresenta múltiplas subseções e subsubseções detalhadas e numeradas até nível 3. Os textos estão associados adequadamente aos seus nós. Não há indicação de continuidade entre páginas para estas seções.
Página apresenta a seção 9.7 'Additional provisions' com texto completo na página e uma seção não numerada 'NOTES' com várias referências. Ambas sem continuidade com outras páginas.
